cobbPlayLink.title: Cobb Can Move Play Link, Download and Latest Version
cobbPlayLink.intro: Play Cobb Can Move from the official itch.io page, either in your browser or through the downloadable build. The current listed release is v1.7, published on May 19, 2026.

cards.name: Official Play Page
cards.description: The official itch.io page is the entry point for both the browser version and the download button.

cards.name: Current Version
cards.description: The latest listed devlog is v1.7, a small balance update to smell, pursuit, and rock behavior.

cards.name: Browser Play
cards.description: The game page includes a Run game option and lists HTML5 as a supported platform.

cards.name: Downloadable Build
cards.description: A Windows download is available from the official page alongside the browser version.

cards.name: Price Model
cards.description: The official download uses itch.io's name-your-own-price model.

cards.name: Platforms
cards.description: The official page lists both browser and Windows platform support.

cards.name: Inputs
cards.description: Keyboard and any gamepad are listed as supported input types.

cards.name: Game Tags
cards.description: Official tags include Survival Horror, Procedural Generation, Singleplayer, and Top-Down.

cobbBeginnerGuide.title: Cobb Can Move Beginner Guide
cobbBeginnerGuide.intro: Learn the basic survival loop before Cobb turns your first run into a short pixel-art tragedy. Cobb Can Move is built around changing rules, short survival runs, resource handling, and reacting to Cobb's abilities.

steps.title: Start from the official itch.io page
steps.description: Launch the browser version or download the current build from the official Cobb Can Move page before playing.

steps.title: Use a supported input type
steps.description: Keyboard and any gamepad are supported. Pick the control method that lets you move carefully, because rushed movement is punished fast.

steps.title: Read the rule text every run
steps.description: The core idea is that the rules change, and level 2 guarantees a sensory rule such as hear, smell, or see.

steps.title: Move carefully around Cobb's senses
steps.description: Sensory rules change how Cobb detects you. Treat sight and smell rules as direct danger signals until you learn how each one feels.

steps.title: Use items without wasting time
steps.description: Item swapping works even when the drop spot sits over a wall, so keep your inventory handling clean and deliberate instead of panic-dropping resources.

steps.title: Learn from death tips
steps.description: Tips appear after dying, making each failed run useful for understanding the next rule or mistake before you restart.

steps.title: Expect rocks to matter
steps.description: A direct rock hit can stun Cobb briefly, and rocks now sound louder, so they are a real defensive tool during chases.

steps.title: Return after beating the story
steps.description: A level select menu unlocks after you beat the game once, letting you replay specific story levels and practice rule combinations.

quickTips: Check the active rule text at the start of every level before moving.
Keep movement deliberate near sight and smell rules.
Use rocks as a defensive tool during long pursuits, not just decoration.
Read the death tip after each failed run before restarting.

cobbRulesAndAbilities.title: Cobb Can Move Rules and Cobb Abilities
cobbRulesAndAbilities.intro: Track the rule changes that shape each run, including sensory rules, pursuit behavior, duplicate handling, and balance changes from recent Cobb Can Move updates.

items.title: Changing Rules
items.summary: Cobb Can Move is a survival horror game where the rules change.
details: Rules alter how each run feels.
Check the active rule text before treating a level like the previous one.

items.title: Sensory Rules
items.summary: Level 2 guarantees a sensory rule: hear, smell, or see.
details: Early runs teach Cobb's detection behavior quickly.
The rule system changes how you should move and react.

items.title: Cobb Can See
items.summary: Sight has been actively balanced across updates, with range adjustments in v1.4 and v1.5.
details: Treat visibility-related rules as major danger modifiers.
Keep more spacing when a sight rule is active.

items.title: Cobb Can Smell
items.summary: v1.7 nerfed smell with less frequent sniffs and less persistence when you are smelled at close range.
details: Smell remains an important sensory rule.
The latest balance update makes smell less relentless than before.

items.title: Long Pursuit Speed
items.summary: Cobb gets faster during long pursuits, with acceleration slightly reduced in v1.7.
details: v1.7 reduced the pressure slightly without removing the pursuit mechanic.

items.title: Reach Rule
items.summary: When reach is active, Cobb takes larger steps but moves with less speed.
details: Reach changes Cobb's movement feel rather than simply making him faster.
Keep more spacing when this rule appears.

items.title: Duplicate Behavior
items.summary: Duplicates now push each other, fixing the issue where they could merge back into one Cobb.
details: Multiple-Cobb situations are clearer and less visually confusing.

items.title: Rock Stun Fix
items.summary: v1.7 fixed rocks sometimes failing to stun Cobb during a pursuit.
details: Rocks became more reliable as a defensive tool.
This matters most when a direct hit lands during a chase.

cobbItemsFurnaceBreaker.title: Cobb Can Move Items, Furnace and Breaker Guide
cobbItemsFurnaceBreaker.intro: Understand the key interactable objects that affect survival, visibility, resource routing, and late-game pressure in Cobb Can Move.

rows.use: Core survival resource connected to the furnace loop.
rows.note: Coal is less likely to spawn right next to the furnace.
rows.takeaway: Expect more movement and routing pressure when collecting coal.

rows.use: Can stun Cobb on a direct hit.
rows.note: Rocks stun Cobb briefly on a direct hit, sound louder, and reliably stun during pursuit after v1.7.
rows.takeaway: Use rocks during dangerous moments instead of leaving them unused.
Carrots are no longer one-and-done after being eaten.

rows.use: Connected to the ending sequence and final task.
rows.note: The final task text changes after placing all four batteries.
rows.takeaway: Track battery progress carefully because the final task changes once all four are placed.

rows.use: Central object tied to the main survival loop and coal routing.
rows.note: Coal spawning pushes players to move farther for fuel.
rows.takeaway: The furnace stays central, but you travel farther for fuel now.

rows.note: The blast furnace in the ending has a larger hitbox so it no longer gets lost behind it.
rows.takeaway: The ending interaction is easier to locate and use after the hitbox change.

rows.use: A visible interactable object you need to recognize during a run.
rows.note: The breaker sprite changed slightly to be more noticeable.
rows.takeaway: Look for the updated breaker appearance when scanning the map.

rows.use: Affects how you swap and manage carried objects.
rows.note: Item swapping works even when the drop spot sits over a wall and shows red.
rows.takeaway: Item swapping is more forgiving, especially near awkward walls.

cobbStoryAndEnding.title: Cobb Can Move Story Mode Levels and Ending
cobbStoryAndEnding.intro: Cobb Can Move story mode is a sequence of rule-changing levels that build toward a final task and an ending. This guide covers progression, batteries, the blast furnace, and the level select menu you unlock after finishing the game.

steps.title: Read the active rule each level
steps.description: Each level changes the rules, so treat every level fresh instead of repeating the same approach from the previous one.

steps.title: Expect sensory rules from level 2
steps.description: Level 2 guarantees a sensory rule such as hear, smell, or see, which is where Cobb's detection behavior becomes the main threat.

steps.title: Gather coal and feed the furnace
steps.description: The early survival loop centers on collecting coal and routing it to the furnace, with coal now spawning farther away to push movement.

steps.title: Watch the breaker
steps.description: The breaker sprite was made more noticeable, so scan the map for it and treat it as a recognizable interactable object.

steps.title: Place all four batteries
steps.description: The final task text changes after placing four batteries, so track your battery progress carefully through the run.

steps.title: Complete the blast furnace ending
steps.description: The blast furnace in the ending has a larger hitbox so the interaction is easier to find and finish without it getting lost.

steps.title: Unlock level select after beating the game
steps.description: A level select menu unlocks after you beat the story once, letting you revisit any story level you want to practice.

steps.title: Replay and refine
steps.description: Use level select to practice difficult rule combinations and sharpen your reaction to each sensory rule.

quickTips: Read the rule text before moving on every level.
Keep four batteries in mind as you push toward the final task.
Use level select after the ending to drill hard rule combos.
Treat the breaker and furnace as reliable landmarks during a run.

cobbEndlessMode.title: Cobb Can Move Endless Mode and Replay Strategy
cobbEndlessMode.intro: Cobb Can Move Endless Mode keeps runs unpredictable because the rules keep changing. This guide explains how Endless works, why later levels raise the pressure, and how to adapt instead of memorizing one fixed route.

cards.name: Endless Starts at Level 3
cards.description: Endless mode begins at level 3, giving you a quick path into repeatable, rule-changing runs.

cards.name: Changing Rules Every Run
cards.description: Rules change from level to level, so no single route stays optimal for long.

cards.name: Rising Pressure
cards.description: Later levels push harder, including faster long pursuits and more demanding sensory rules.

cards.name: Adapt Over Memorize
cards.description: Win by reacting to the active rule instead of repeating a fixed path that worked once.

cards.name: Level Select After Story
cards.description: Beating the story unlocks level select, useful for practicing rule combinations before long Endless pushes.

cards.name: Use Items to Extend Runs
cards.description: Rocks, carrots, and clean coal routing all help you survive longer as pressure ramps up.

cobbUpdatesPatchNotes.title: Cobb Can Move Updates and Patch Notes
cobbUpdatesPatchNotes.intro: Cobb Can Move received several 2026 updates after renewed player attention. This timeline tracks version notes, balance changes, quality-of-life additions, and bug fixes from the official devlog.

entries.title: v1.7
entries.description: Nerfed smell with less frequent sniffs, slightly reduced pursuit acceleration, and fixed rocks sometimes failing to stun Cobb during pursuit.

entries.title: v1.6
entries.description: Made Cobb faster in long pursuits, changed reach to larger steps with less speed, fixed duplicates merging, moved coal spawns away from the furnace, made rocks stun Cobb on a direct hit, enlarged the blast furnace hitbox, and improved international keyboard layouts.

entries.title: v1.5
entries.description: Adjusted the Cobb Can See range again and added a level select menu that unlocks after beating the game.

entries.title: v1.4
entries.description: Guaranteed a sensory rule on level 2, slightly nerfed Cobb Can See, allowed item swapping over walls, added death tips, and changed the final task text after placing four batteries.

entries.title: v1.3
entries.description: Started Endless mode at level 3, made carrots respawn after being eaten, and tweaked the breaker sprite to be more noticeable.

cobbCodesAndStatus.title: Cobb Can Move Codes, Rewards and Official Status
cobbCodesAndStatus.intro: The official Cobb Can Move itch.io page does not show a public redeem-code system. This guide points you to the real ways to get the game and stay current: the name-your-own-price download and the official devlog for updates.

cards.name: No Public Code System
cards.description: The official page does not list a redeem-code system, so there are no active Cobb Can Move codes to enter.

cards.name: Name Your Own Price
cards.description: The official download uses itch.io's name-your-own-price model, including a free option, so price is never a barrier to playing.

cards.name: Official itch.io Page
cards.description: abho.itch.io/cobb-can-move is the official entry point for both the browser version and the Windows download.

cards.name: Real Updates on the Devlog
cards.description: Version notes, balance changes, and bug fixes all appear on the official devlog rather than in any code drop.

cards.name: Ignore Fake Code Bait
cards.description: Third-party sites advertising active codes are not official, so treat them as noise and check the devlog instead.

cards.name: Stay Current in the Community
cards.description: Join the official Discord for release news and devlog updates so you never miss a real Cobb Can Move change.

faq.title: Cobb Can Move
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Cobb Can Move

questions.question: Where can I play Cobb Can Move?
questions.answer: You can play Cobb Can Move on the official itch.io page at abho.itch.io/cobb-can-move, either in your browser through the HTML5 version or by downloading the Windows build.

questions.question: Is Cobb Can Move free?
questions.answer: Cobb Can Move uses itch.io's name-your-own-price model, which includes a free option, so you can play without paying unless you want to support the developer.

questions.question: Are there active Cobb Can Move codes?
questions.answer: No. The official Cobb Can Move page does not list a redeem-code system, so there are no active codes to enter. Real updates arrive through the official devlog.

questions.question: What platforms and inputs does Cobb Can Move support?
questions.answer: Cobb Can Move supports HTML5 and Windows, with keyboard and gamepad input listed on the official page.

questions.question: What is the latest version of Cobb Can Move?
questions.answer: The latest listed version of Cobb Can Move is v1.7, published on May 19, 2026, which adjusted smell, pursuit acceleration, and rock stun behavior.

questions.question: How do Cobb Can Move rules work?
questions.answer: In Cobb Can Move the rules change each level, and level 2 guarantees a sensory rule such as hear, smell, or see, so each run demands a different approach.
